front: Where does the CPU store data and instructions temporarily?
Why are registers used to store data and instructions?
back: Because they are very fast and allow quick access by the CPU.

front: Which register stores the address of the next instruction?
Which register stores the address being accessed in memory?
Which register stores the data or instruction fetched from memory?
Which register stores the current instruction being processed?
Which register stores the result of processing?
Why is it important to store addresses as well as data?
back: So the CPU knows where to fetch data from or where to store results in memory.
